question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with a maximally-sized hookIds collection (up to MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS) targeting a single ownerId, causing the mirror node database to execute an unbounded IN-clause query that exhausts DB connection pool resources, thereby preventing the mirror node from processing and relaying incoming gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user supply lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(), causing a full table scan that locks DB read resources and starves the importer pipeline of DB connections needed to persist incoming gossip transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user enumerate all valid ownerIds by iterating account IDs in the path parameter of GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks and observing non-empty vs empty responses from findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), then use that knowledge to craft targeted hook-based gossip suppression attacks against specific high-value accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user send thousands of concurrent requests to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() with wide ranges, causing a thread pool exhaustion in the REST Java service that prevents the mirror node from serving transaction status queries needed by gossip participants to confirm transaction propagation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user craft a hookIds collection containing duplicate values to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), causing the Spring Data JPA query to generate a malformed or oversized SQL IN clause that triggers a DB error, disrupting the mirror node's ability to record hook-related gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user supply lowerBound greater than upperBound to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(), and if the repository does not validate this inversion, does it produce an always-empty or undefined SQL range that could be exploited to mask hook deletions and allow gossip of transactions that should have been blocked by active hooks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user pass hookId values of 0 or negative numbers to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), bypassing the @Positive validation at the controller layer if the parameter is parsed differently, causing the repository to return hooks that should not be visible and leaking hook metadata that reveals which transactions are subject to hook-based gossip filtering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a deleted=false filter in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to retrieve soft-deleted hooks, obtaining stale hook metadata that incorrectly indicates active gossip-blocking hooks on accounts, causing clients to incorrectly believe certain transactions will be intercepted and suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() returns deleted hooks (no deleted=false filter) to observe hook lifecycle events and infer the timing of gossip-blocking hook activations and deactivations on target accounts, enabling a timing attack to submit transactions during the window when hooks are inactive?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user abuse the Pageable parameter passed to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() by manipulating the sort direction to force a descending full-index scan on the hook table, causing significantly higher I/O than ascending scans and degrading mirror node performance to the point where gossip transaction ingestion is delayed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user send a request where the hookIds collection passed to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() contains the maximum allowed number of entries (MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS), and if this limit is not enforced at the repository layer, does it allow a SQL IN clause large enough to exceed database query plan limits, causing query failures that corrupt hook state visibility for gossip transaction validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the PagingAndSortingRepository interface to request an unpaged result set (Pageable.unpaged()) through a crafted request, bypassing the limit=100 cap enforced at the controller layer, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to return all hooks in the range and exhausting JVM heap memory, which crashes the REST service and prevents gossip transaction status reporting?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the composite primary key (ownerId, hookId) structure of the Hook entity to craft requests that cause index contention in the hook table during concurrent hook ingestion from gossip transactions, preventing the importer from writing new hook records and stalling gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the timestamp_range field in the Hook entity (returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()) to infer the exact consensus timestamps of gossip transactions that created or modified hooks, enabling replay or front-running attacks against those transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the adminKey field exposed in Hook responses (populated via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()) to extract cryptographic key material that could be used to forge hook management transactions, allowing the attacker to delete or modify hooks that are preventing gossip of certain transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the extensionPoint field (e.g., ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK) returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify which specific transaction types (e.g., CryptoTransfer allowance operations) are subject to hook interception, then craft transactions that bypass those extension points to avoid gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user perform a slow-loris style attack by opening many simultaneous long-running connections to the hooks endpoint backed by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), each holding a DB connection open, exhausting the connection pool and preventing the importer from persisting gossip transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the contractId field returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify the EVM contract (0.0.365 system contract) associated with hook execution, and then craft ContractCall transactions targeting that contract to interfere with hook execution and prevent gossip of hook-triggered child transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the createdTimestamp field exposed via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to correlate hook creation events with specific gossip transactions, enabling the attacker to identify and target the exact transactions that established gossip-blocking hooks for subsequent suppression?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() does not filter by hook type (HookType.EVM) to retrieve hooks of any type, and if future hook types are added that have different gossip-blocking semantics, does this allow the attacker to enumerate and exploit those hooks before defenses are updated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the PagingAndSortingRepository's findAll() method (inherited but not restricted) to retrieve all hooks across all owners without ownerId filtering, exposing the complete set of gossip-blocking hooks on the network and enabling targeted attacks against accounts with active hooks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the hookId range query in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to infer the total number of hooks registered for an account by performing binary search queries, revealing the scale of gossip-blocking hook infrastructure on high-value accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Spring Data JPA derived query mechanism in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to trigger N+1 query problems when the Hook entity has lazy-loaded associations, causing excessive DB queries that degrade performance and delay gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the inclusive range semantics of findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() (BETWEEN is inclusive on both ends) combined with Long.MAX_VALUE as upperBound to cause integer overflow or unexpected behavior in the SQL BETWEEN clause, returning incorrect hook sets that misrepresent which transactions are subject to gossip blocking?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the EVM address format of the ownerId path parameter (0x-prefixed hex) to resolve to a different account than the numeric ID format, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to return hooks for an unintended account and leaking gossip-blocking hook configurations?
